extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, whose condition is the conjunction of all six flags (asthma, elderly, pregnancy, seizure_history, prior_gi_bleed, immunosuppressed). No other rule sets rec_drug_a, so in any input not satisfying all six, rec_drug_a defaults to false and the conjunction (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) is trivially false. In the only scenario where rec_drug_a can be true (all six flags true), rec_drug_b has two matching rules: r1 (priority 90, rec_drug_b := false) and r2 (priority 50, rec_drug_b := true). Conflict resolution picks the highest-priority matching rule per output, so r1 (90 > 50) wins and rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is forced false, and the two are never jointly true. The property holds over the entire input space.
